content.full_text:
April 16, 1991

Ms. Carla Wardlow  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street  
Suite 610  
Sacramento, California 95814  

Re: Receipt of Contributions  

Dear Ms. Wardlow:

This is to confirm our telephone conversation on April 16, 1991, in which we discussed a question concerning depositing contributions received by a political committee. As I stated, the committee only now discovered that certain contributions had been sent to the committee between October and December of 1990. I asked you whether there is any requirement that checks be deposited by a specified time after receipt so that it would present any problem now to deposit the checks.

You stated that there is no specification in the statutes or regulations that checks must be deposited by a specified time after receipt. As a result, you stated that the checks may still be deposited. Of course, you pointed out that Form 405 amendments must be filed to reflect the receipt of the checks.

Please let me know immediately if I have misstated our conversation in any way. Thank you very much for your cooperation and courtesy.

Very truly yours,

Ronald B. Turovsky  
Manatt, Phelps & Phillips
